values.value: Posts messages to a Slack channel. Supports two modes: Incoming Webhook (paste a webhook URL, no auth setup needed, one channel per service) or Web API via Named Credential (Bot Token auth, dynamic channel per recipient, richer response data including message ts for threading).

## Slack setup
Pick either **Incoming Webhook mode** (simpler, one channel per service) or **Web API mode via Named Credential** (richer response data, dynamic channel per recipient).
### Incoming Webhook mode
1. In Slack, go to `https://api.slack.com/apps` -> **Create New App** -> **From scratch** 2. Under **Incoming Webhooks**, activate them and click **Add New Webhook to Workspace** 3. Pick the target channel, authorize 4. Copy the webhook URL (starts with `https://hooks.slack.com/services/...`) 5. Paste it into this service's `Webhook URL` field 6. On the recipient, leave `Channel Identifier` blank - the webhook is already channel-scoped
### Web API mode via Named Credential
1. In Slack, create a Slack app as above 2. Under **OAuth & Permissions** -> **Bot Token Scopes**, add `chat:write` (and `chat:write.public` to post in channels without inviting the bot) 3. **Install to Workspace**, copy the Bot User OAuth Token (starts with `xoxb-`) 4. In Salesforce Setup -> **External Credentials** -> New with Custom auth protocol 5. Add a **Custom Header**: Name `Authorization`, Value `Bearer <your xoxb- token>` 6. Add a **Principal** (any name), enable it 7. Setup -> **Named Credentials** -> New with URL `https://slack.com/api`, referencing the External Credential, `Generate Authorization Header` checked 8. Assign the `LoggerNotificationsPluginAdmin` permission set to the **Automated Process user** (required for async notifier dispatch) 9. Paste the Named Credential's developer name into this service's `Named Credential Developer Name` field 10. On each recipient, populate `Channel Identifier` with the Slack channel ID (e.g. `C0123ABCD456`)
